ksa_text: Ability to design data systems, databases, and integration mechanisms, determining data requirements and managing data flows.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of infrastructure supporting information technology (IT) for safety, performance, and reliability.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of infrastructure supporting information technology (IT) for safety, performance, and reliability.
Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Technical Foundations falls outside the primary competency scope of the Director of AI Ethics/Responsible AI. This ability KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is Technical Foundations truly out-of-scope for the Director of AI Ethics/Responsible AI? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list Technical Foundations as a core or recommended competency for the Director of AI Ethics/Responsible AI. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms Technical Foundations falls outside the established competency profile for the Director of AI Ethics/Responsible AI. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Technical Foundations is outside the competency scope of the Director of AI Ethics/Responsible AI. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
